summary:
The correct exception — i.e., not a sign of hydrocephalus—is “depresed fontanelle” because true hydrocephalus presents with tense/bulging fontanels due to increased pressure; crack post sign refers to hemorrhagic conditions unrelated to CSF buildup.

\boxed{\text{d}}

\boxed{\text{d}}. Answer choice: C
